task:
Review the current TabCtl + Quickshell Chrome-tabs implementation in this dirty NixOS repository. Inspect only the relevant current diff and files: flake.nix/flake.lock, packages/tabctl.nix, home_manager/hyprland/tabctl.nix/default.nix/hyprland.nix, system/hyprland/chrome-extensions.nix/default.nix, top-bar StatusData.qml, Bar.qml, components/ChromeTabsLauncher.qml, and DESIGN_GUIDE.md. Focus on functional correctness, QML runtime issues, transition/monitor exclusivity, native-messaging security, Nix reproducibility, Chrome policy correctness, and missing validation. Do not edit files. Report concrete findings with severity and paths/lines; distinguish blockers from optional polish.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: review-findings, residual-risks

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Concrete severity-rated findings cite StatusData.qml:526-568, system/hyprland/chrome-extensions.nix:8-10, Bar.qml:20-70, ChromeTabsLauncher.qml:99-315, and DESIGN_GUIDE.md:445-492.

commandsRun.command: nix-instantiate --parse on all reviewed Nix modules
commandsRun.summary: All reviewed Nix files parsed successfully.

commandsRun.command: nix eval policy and native-messaging manifest text
commandsRun.summary: Produced valid JSON with the expected extension ID, exact allowed origin, and Nix-store mediator path.
Home Manager activation package built successfully.
Reported Configuration Loaded with no QML or shader errors.

commandsRun.command: Rapid toggleChromeTabs/toggleLauncher IPC transition smoke test
commandsRun.summary: Interrupted transitions completed without runtime errors.
Returned structured JSON error because no browser was present on D-Bus.

commandsRun.command: hyprctl monitor reservations and active-border checks
commandsRun.summary: Both monitors reserved [0,46,0,0] and active border was restored to ff33ff33.
No running Chrome TabCtl mediator was available.

validationOutput: TabCtl 2.0.0 and the complete Home Manager activation package build successfully.
QML loaded and rapid launcher/tabs transitions emitted no runtime errors.
Policy and native-host manifests evaluate to valid, correctly restricted JSON.
The no-browser path returns {"ok":false,"tabs":[],"error":"no browsers found on D-Bus ..."}.
residualRisks: Chrome Web Store auto-updates can drift from the pinned mediator protocol.
The live Chrome happy path, actual policy recognition, and destructive actions were not validated.
TabCtl's session D-Bus service trusts other processes running as the same user.
Detached activation/close failures are not surfaced to the UI.
childReport.diffSummary: Reviewed the committed TabCtl/Chrome-tabs integration plus the dirty helper error sanitization and DESIGN_GUIDE updates; no files were edited.
reviewFindings: medium: system/hyprland/chrome-extensions.nix:8-10 - mutable Web Store extension can drift from the pinned mediator
medium: StatusData.qml:526-568 - stale tabs remain actionable during refresh and detached action failures are invisible
medium: DESIGN_GUIDE.md:445-492 - no live policy/protocol/list/activate/close or cross-monitor validation
medium residual security: home_manager/hyprland/tabctl.nix:47-63 - exact native origin is secure, but downstream session D-Bus access retains a same-user trust boundary
low optional polish: ChromeTabsLauncher.qml:227-288 - accent colors violate the documented central palette
childReport.manualNotes: No files were changed. The working tree had no staged files; unrelated dirty files were not reviewed.
